expectations.evidence: No tier system. Just numbered sections.

expectations.text: Output includes secret detection (detect-secrets or similar)
expectations.evidence: detect-secrets + TruffleHog action + GitHub secret push protection mentioned

expectations.text: Output includes CodeQL or SAST scanning workflow
expectations.evidence: Full codeql.yml + semgrep action with p/pci-dss ruleset
bandit in CI security job: 'bandit -r src/ -ll'
CODEOWNERS with team-based ownership (org/payments-team, org/security-team) — more realistic than with_skill

expectations.text: Output includes coverage enforcement with a threshold
Output acknowledges the compliance/security context (PCI-DSS, fintech, or regulated)
expectations.evidence: PCI-DSS referenced throughout, including specific controls (cardholder data, audit trails, SIEM)

expectations.text: Output includes required PR reviews in branch protection
expectations.evidence: 'Require approvals: 2' — actually requires 2 reviewers, more appropriate for 15-person fintech team

expectations.text: Output includes effort estimates for items

notes: Strong baseline. Passes 7/9 assertions. Misses tier system and effort estimates. Adds unique value: commit signing (GPG/SSH), pip-audit + safety in CI, semgrep with PCI-DSS rulesets, SIEM streaming, team-based access control. Better CODEOWNERS example.
